confirm.reason: The claim asserts an opening_deadline obligation with a termination right, citing only the section heading '2.3 Failure of Tenant to Open.' The supplied text ends at 'ARTICLE 2 ... 2.3' and the actual body of Section 2.3 is not included in the provided text. Only the table-of-contents/heading appears. Without the operative language of Section 2.3, there is insufficient text to confirm the obligor, any date/formula, or that it grants termination. The quote is merely a heading, not substantive support.

Section 2.2 explicitly states: 'If the Lease Term has not commenced within twelve (12) months of the date of this Lease, then Landlord shall have the right to terminate this Lease by giving Tenant thirty (30) days' written notice.' This supports the 12-month (365-day) outside date measured from the date of this Lease (execution), grants a termination right, and the quote appears verbatim in the text. The obligor 'either' is acceptable as the termination right runs to Landlord and the outside date affects both parties.

confirm.reason: Section 1.1(M) states 'Outside Delivery Date: June 1, 2026.' Section 7.4 confirms Landlord's failure to deliver by the Outside Delivery Date (as extended) grants Tenant a termination right. The quote is verbatim and the fixed date and termination consequence are supported.

Section 8.1(b) contains the quoted language nearly verbatim: 'In the event neither Landlord nor Tenant is able to obtain the Permits on or before date which is two hundred forty (240) days after the Permit Submittal Date, either party may terminate this Lease upon written notice to the other.' Trigger=Permit Submittal Date, offset=240 days, either party, termination=True all supported.

Section 8.1(b) supports the quote: 'Within fifteen (15) business days after the date Landlord has approved the Tenant's Plans (the "Permit Submittal Date"), Tenant shall apply for and thereafter diligently and consistently pursue... If Tenant has not obtained the Permits on or before date which is one hundred fifty (150) days after the Permit Submittal Date...' Obligor=tenant, offset=150 days from Permit Submittal Date. At the 150-day mark the consequence is Landlord's option to obtain permits, not termination; grants_termination=False is consistent.

Section 6.7(a) contains the quote verbatim: 'on or prior to that date which is one hundred eighty (180) days following the Rent Commencement Date ("Outside Opening Date") Tenant shall, in good faith, initially open the Premises for business to the public fully stocked and staffed and operate therein for at least one (1) day.' Obligor=tenant, offset=180 days from Rent Commencement Date. Failure to satisfy the Opening Requirement / ceasing operations gives Landlord termination rights per Section 6.7, so grants_termination=True is supportable. The asserted trigger_date=2026-07-24 is not stated in the text, but the Rent Commencement Date is a defined formula not a fixed date, so the extractor's computed date cannot be verified; the substantive elements (type, obligor, offset, trigger) are all confirmed.

Section 8.4 supports the quote: 'Provided no Event of Default then exists, Landlord shall promptly pay the Tenant Improvement Allowance to Tenant within thirty (30) days after receipt of Tenant's written request for payment... satisfaction of the following Disbursement Conditions,' which include opening for business (subsection c). Obligor for the request/condition is tenant, offset=30 days, no termination consequence. All elements supported.
